title: Roadmap assumptions and thesis lock for 1.0.0
body_lines: Lock the product thesis, non-goals, and roadmap assumptions before prototype work begins.
- North star statement for the product.
- Assumptions that later milestones are allowed to rely on.
- The roadmap has a stable thesis that later issues can cite.
- The difference between 0.0.x exploration and 0.1.0 MVP is explicit.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: roadmap north star and version model sections.

title: Release gates from 0.1.0 to 1.0.0
body_lines: Capture the brutal capability gates that determine whether the roadmap can move from MVP toward 1.0.0.
- Short rationale for why each gate matters.
- Every major roadmap jump has a clear go or no-go condition.
- The gates align with the conversation source and can be referenced in milestone epics.

title: Epic: five-lane workflow operations
body_lines: Define the operating model for one overseer and five Spark lanes so work can proceed in isolated worktrees with deterministic handoff and gates.
- PR flow from lane work to mainline.
- A contributor can describe the full lane lifecycle from assignment to merge.
- The workflow is explicit enough to prevent cross-lane collisions and silent handoff gaps.
- User instruction: operate the five-lane workflow in worktrees with no human in the loop.

title: Define overseer intake, lane assignment, and conflict-resolution rules
body_lines: Define how the overseer receives work, assigns it to the five Spark lanes, and resolves overlap or drift.
- Intake criteria for new work items.
- Lane assignment rules across Spark 1 through Spark 5.
- Conflict-resolution and escalation rules when lanes collide or block each other.
- Drift detection or handoff checklist for stalled work.
- The overseer can route work without ad hoc judgment calls.
- Lane ownership is unambiguous when issues are created or reassigned.
- Blocked by: Epic: five-lane workflow operations
- `AGENTS.md`: overseer responsibility and lane ownership rules.

title: Standardize worktree setup, branch naming, and lane isolation
body_lines: Define the canonical worktree layout and branch naming so each Spark lane stays isolated from the others.
- Worktree path convention for each lane.
- Branch naming convention for lane-scoped work.
- Rules that prevent one lane from overwriting another lane's files or branch state.
- Each lane can work independently in its own git worktree.
- The worktree rules are specific enough to be automated later.
- Blocked by: Epic: five-lane workflow operations

title: Codify PR flow from lane worktree to mainline
body_lines: Define the path from a lane-local worktree to a reviewable pull request and a clean merge back to mainline.
- Required PR metadata and summary expectations.
- Handoff rules from Spark lane completion to overseer review.
- Lane work always reaches mainline through the same documented PR path.
- PRs make lane ownership and validation status obvious.
- Depends on: Standardize worktree setup, branch naming, and lane isolation

title: Codify CI, auto-review, and merge gate behavior
body_lines: Define the automated validation path that decides whether lane work can merge without manual intervention.
- Merge-gate rules for passing or rejecting a PR.
- Passing work can move through review and merge without a human decision in the normal path.
- Depends on: Codify PR flow from lane worktree to mainline
- `AGENTS.md`: CI, auto-review, and no-human merge path.

title: Epic: 0.0.0 foundations
body_lines: Define the product language, doctrine, entity model, and event model so later milestones do not drift conceptually.
- The system can be described in precise entities and flows rather than vibes.
- Later milestone issues can reference frozen terms and schemas.

title: Define product doctrine and MVP proof criteria
body_lines: Write the canonical short doctrine docs for thesis, reward and punishment philosophy, pacing philosophy, and MVP proof criteria.
- A new contributor can explain what the product is and what 0.1.0 must prove.
- The doctrine explicitly forbids breadth-first fake Duolingo behavior.

title: Define ontology v0 for concepts, traps, regions, bosses, questions and state
body_lines: Define the first-pass entity model for content, graph nodes, combat objects, player mastery, and pace.
- Core entities such as Concept, TrapPattern, Region, Boss, Question, Attack, and UserMasteryState.
- First-pass state model including new, learned, fragile, mastered, corrupted, and known mistake.
- Core entities and states are stable enough to support annotation, map, combat, and telemetry workstreams.
- The ontology is specific enough to stop naming drift.

title: Define graph grammar and map projection rules
body_lines: Formalize node types, edge types, clustering rules, submap recursion, and how concept structure becomes a navigable map.
- The map system has explicit rules for content-to-space translation.
- Recursive submaps are supported at the contract level from the start.
- Depends on: Define ontology v0 for concepts, traps, regions, bosses, questions and state

title: Define narrative bible v0 for Road Master and Chapter I
body_lines: Lock the Road Master fantasy, mentor tone, chapter arc, first region identity, and first boss identity.
- First region theme and first boss identity.
- Narrative work later in the roadmap can stay sparse, severe, and consistent.
- Chapter I has a stable framing before content authoring begins.

title: Define instrumentation schema v0 and mandatory event taxonomy
body_lines: Define the events and payloads needed to understand question performance, pacing, flashbacks, and session outcomes.
- Mandatory event list from session start through boss clear or fail.
- Payload fields for question, trap, concept, HP, pace, and flashback state.
- The event list covers the minimum set called out in the source conversation.
- Later telemetry work can instrument systems without renaming events.
- Depends on: Define ontology v0 for concepts, traps, regions, bosses, questions and state
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 0.0.0 Instrumentation schema v0 and 0.1.0 telemetry section.

title: Epic: 0.0.1 content annotation prototype
body_lines: Prove that raw driving exam questions can be transformed into the internal concept and trap model.
- Internal editor for inspection and edits.
- A raw question can be compiled into game content using a consistent schema.
- The seed dataset is large enough to test map, combat, and pacing contracts.

title: Design annotation schema for question-to-concept and trap mapping
body_lines: Define how each question records concepts, trap patterns, difficulty, attack tier, region, analogies, boss compatibility, and known-ground suitability.
- Rules for primary and secondary concept assignment.
- Rules for trap naming and selection.
- Annotators can classify a question without inventing new fields.
- The schema supports future content scaling and analytics.
- Depends on: Define ontology v0 for concepts, traps, regions, bosses, questions and state

title: Annotate seed dataset of 50-100 questions for one region
body_lines: Manually annotate the first small question corpus for a single region and boss domain.
- The dataset is sufficient to drive a first map region and first combat encounters.
- Annotation consistency is high enough to expose schema gaps early.
- Depends on: Design annotation schema for question-to-concept and trap mapping

title: Build internal content editor for inspection and edits
body_lines: Build a rough internal editor that makes it possible to inspect and edit content objects without touching raw files directly.
- Internal editor or inspector for annotated content objects.
- Basic support for creating, editing, and reviewing annotation metadata.
- Content can be inspected without custom scripts per object type.
- The editor is good enough to support manual scaling in later milestones.
- Depends on: Design annotation schema for question-to-concept and trap mapping

title: Epic: 0.0.2 pure combat prototype
body_lines: Prove the question-as-attack loop without the complexity of the world map.
- One encounter is more fun than a flat test.
- Failure and low-HP states feel meaningful rather than cosmetic.

title: Implement encounter loop with telegraphed attacks, HP and failure states
body_lines: Build the basic encounter flow where questions arrive as attacks with telegraph tiers and wrong answers reduce HP.
- Players can enter a prototype encounter, answer questions, take damage, and fail.
- The telegraph tier is visible before answer submission.
- Depends on: Define ontology v0 for concepts, traps, regions, bosses, questions and state

title: Implement answer resolution, damage, reward and retry logic
body_lines: Resolve answers into correctness, damage, small rewards, bigger clear rewards, and retry loops.
- Wrong answers sting and encounter clears feel satisfying.
- Retry behavior keeps the prototype loop intact rather than restarting the whole experience.
- Depends on: Implement encounter loop with telegraphed attacks, HP and failure states

title: Epic: 0.0.3 pacing prototype
body_lines: Turn the combat loop into a session with rhythm, spikes, recovery, and clutch moments.
- Sessions have shape rather than flat difficulty.
- The pacing system can be tuned independently from raw content breadth.

title: Implement deterministic pacing state machine for flow, pressure, danger, recovery, clutch and repair
body_lines: Implement the first deterministic rules for pace states and transitions using recent run history and answer performance.
- Configurable thresholds for the first prototype.
- The pacing logic is explicit, testable, and not hardcoded into presentation code.
- Pace state changes can be instrumented later without renaming concepts.
- Depends on: Implement encounter loop with telegraphed attacks, HP and failure states

title: Add low-HP escalation, hidden recovery injections and boss phase rhythm
body_lines: Use the pacing state machine to drive low-HP intensity, recovery windows, and boss-phase transitions.
- Danger and recovery states are felt clearly during a session.
- Boss pacing no longer feels like a flat queue of questions.
- Depends on: Implement deterministic pacing state machine for flow, pressure, danger, recovery, clutch and repair

title: Epic: 0.0.4 memory palace prototype
body_lines: Prove that the product can encode concepts spatially and recall them through flashbacks and reclaim loops.
- A player can say a concept lived in a specific place.
- The map is functional memory structure, not just decoration.

title: Implement first map region with nodes, navigation and place-linked concepts
body_lines: Build the first map region with nodes that represent content neighborhoods and expose basic navigation.
- The map can be navigated as a place rather than as a list of tests.
- Concepts are visibly associated with locations in the region.
- Depends on: Define graph grammar and map projection rules

title: Implement known-mistake flashback and reclaim loop
body_lines: Detect known-ground mistakes, flash back to the relevant place, and schedule reclaim shortly afterward.
- A known-ground miss can reactivate the remembered place.
- Reclaim feels like a recovery mechanic rather than generic review.
- Depends on: Implement first map region with nodes, navigation and place-linked concepts
- Depends on: Implement deterministic pacing state machine for flow, pressure, danger, recovery, clutch and repair

title: Epic: 0.0.5 narrative vertical slice
body_lines: Wrap the first region in the Road Master shell so the prototype feels like a campaign instead of mechanics with lore pasted on later.
- First boss introduction and defeat ritual.
- The prototype feels like a coherent campaign chapter.
- Tone rules are stable enough for later content writing.

title: Author Road Master intro, mentor voice v0 and first region shell
body_lines: Write the initial Road Master framing, define mentor tone, and give the first region a distinct identity.
- Mentor tone and first voice pass.
- The opening of Chapter I establishes severe, sparse, ceremonial tone.
- The first region has a clear narrative role before the boss appears.
- Depends on: Define narrative bible v0 for Road Master and Chapter I

title: Implement first boss intro, defeat ritual and region conquered state
body_lines: Define the first boss encounter framing, the defeat ritual, and the feeling of conquering the region.
- Boss entry and victory have distinct emotional identity.
- The prototype can end a region with a campaign-like state change.
- Depends on: Author Road Master intro, mentor voice v0 and first region shell

title: Epic: 0.0.6 integrated vertical slice
body_lines: Combine the graph, map, combat, pacing, flashback, and narrative systems into one polished internal slice.
- Concept graph, map reveal, combat, flashback, reclaim, boss clear, and region conquest.
- Internal playtesters recognize the product as a real thing rather than a concept deck.
- The slice demonstrates the core thesis end to end.

title: Integrate graph, map, combat, pacing, flashback and narrative into one playable region
body_lines: Join the separate prototype streams into one playable region with a boss, reclaim loop, and conquest state.
- One region and one boss wired end to end.
- Map reveal, combat loop, flashback, reclaim, boss clear, and region conquest.
- A single session can move from map navigation to boss fight to conquest without fake placeholders.
- The integrated loop is stable enough to instrument and balance next.
- Depends on: Epic: 0.0.2 pure combat prototype
- Depends on: Epic: 0.0.4 memory palace prototype
- Depends on: Epic: 0.0.5 narrative vertical slice

title: Epic: 0.0.7 telemetry and balancing
body_lines: Measure the integrated slice and use evidence to tune it instead of arguing from intuition.
- Completion, quit, retry, and survival metrics.
- Balancing decisions can be tied to real prototype data.
- The slice exposes where players die, quit, and recover.

title: Wire telemetry dashboards or core balancing queries for the vertical slice
body_lines: Capture the minimum analytical surface needed to understand player exits, failures, and recoveries in the first playable slice.
- Boss retry and low-HP survival queries.
- Flashback trigger and reclaim success queries.
- The prototype can answer the minimum analytics questions listed in the roadmap.
- Telemetry is structured enough for future dashboards or exports.
- Depends on: Define instrumentation schema v0 and mandatory event taxonomy
- Depends on: Integrate graph, map, combat, pacing, flashback and narrative into one playable region

title: Tune vertical-slice metrics using completion, retry, fail and reclaim data
body_lines: Use the collected data to adjust difficulty, pacing, flashback behavior, and content fairness in the first slice.
- Tuning pass for difficulty and pacing.
- Fixes for unfair spikes and overperforming traps.
- Documented reasons for significant balance changes.
- The slice can be tuned with evidence instead of anecdote.
- Outlier questions and unfair punishments are explicitly addressed.
- Depends on: Wire telemetry dashboards or core balancing queries for the vertical slice

title: Epic: 0.0.8 content production pass
body_lines: Make sure content can be produced consistently by humans before attempting broader scale.
- Authoring templates for analogies, bosses, explanations, and reclaim flows.
- A human can produce new content using repeatable rules.
- Content quality no longer depends on one-off intuition.

title: Define annotation workflow, QA checklist and naming schemes
body_lines: Codify how content is created, reviewed, and named so new regions can be authored consistently.
- The workflow is explicit enough for another contributor to use without verbal handholding.
- Trap and content names stay stable across later milestones.
- Depends on: Design annotation schema for question-to-concept and trap mapping

title: Define reusable templates for analogies, bosses, explanations and reclaim flows
body_lines: Create reusable templates for authored content so new chapters can keep the same pedagogical and dramatic structure.
- New content follows a recognizable structure rather than improvised formatting.
- The templates support both pedagogy and the Road Master tone.
- Depends on: Define annotation workflow, QA checklist and naming schemes

title: Epic: 0.0.9 MVP hardening
body_lines: Cut scope, harden the first-time-user experience, and make the MVP safe to publish to real users.
- The MVP can be shown to real users without embarrassment.
- Non-essential features have been cut rather than partially shipped.

title: Implement onboarding, persistence and first-time user path
body_lines: Build the minimum onboarding, persistence, and session continuity required for a first external MVP.
- New users can enter, leave, and return without losing the chapter structure.
- Persistence is reliable enough for a real beta.
- Depends on: Integrate graph, map, combat, pacing, flashback and narrative into one playable region

title: Run MVP feature freeze and hardening pass
body_lines: Decide what enters 0.1.0, cut the rest, and harden the surviving feature set before external release.
- Risk register for release candidate quality.
- The 0.1.0 scope is explicit and defended against late feature creep.
- Critical bugs and embarrassing gaps have owners or fixes.
- Depends on: Implement onboarding, persistence and first-time user path

title: Epic: 0.1.0 MVP
body_lines: Ship Road Master: Crossing Fields Chapter I as a deep vertical slice that proves the thesis publicly or in a real beta.
- One memorable region with one recursive submap and one iconic boss.
- Map, combat, pacing, flashback, explanation, narrative, and telemetry loops.
- Crossing Fields content pack and Right-of-Way Beast boss.
- A player can start the campaign, fight through the chapter, trigger flashback and reclaim, beat the boss, and conquer the region.
- The MVP feels more like a game than study and proves the deep-not-broad strategy.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 0.1.0 roadmap, chosen direction, and Codex-ready execution doc.

title: Freeze contracts for schemas, state machines, content model and event names
body_lines: Lock the contracts that let parallel workstreams proceed without chaos: enums, types, state shapes, content model, and event names.
- Run state and player state shapes.
- Parallel workstreams can proceed with stable contracts.
- Major renames should not be needed after this milestone.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: Milestone 1 Contracts frozen and section 4 Domain contracts.

title: Build dummy-data vertical slice for map, combat, pacing and boss flow
body_lines: Prove the integration path early by running the core loop against fake nodes, fake questions, and dummy region identifiers.
- A fake region can be navigated and fought from end to end.
- Real chapter content can later drop into the same contracts.
- Depends on: Freeze contracts for schemas, state machines, content model and event names
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: Milestone 2 Fake-data integration and M2 Dummy vertical slice.

title: Author Chapter I Crossing Fields content pack
body_lines: Author the Chapter I content pack with Crossing Fields, Four-Way Labyrinth, Right-of-Way Beast, named trap patterns, analogies, and explanations.
- Right-of-Way Beast and mentor or boss lines.
- Real content can drive the dummy systems without schema changes.
- The chapter expresses the chosen deep 0.1.0 direction clearly.
- Depends on: Freeze contracts for schemas, state machines, content model and event names
- Depends on: Define reusable templates for analogies, bosses, explanations and reclaim flows
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 0.1.0 chosen direction, section 5 Chapter 1 content pack, and M3 Chapter 1 content pack.

title: Implement memory mechanics for known-ground slips and reclaim
body_lines: Implement known-ground slip detection, flashback overlays, reclaim scheduling, and fragile or corrupted state marking for Chapter I.
- Known mistakes feel spatial and punitive.
- Flashback and reclaim work on real chapter content rather than only dummy data.
- Depends on: Build dummy-data vertical slice for map, combat, pacing and boss flow
- Depends on: Author Chapter I Crossing Fields content pack
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: M4 Memory mechanics and section 7.4 Memory / flashback system.

title: Add narrative and audiovisual pass for Chapter I
body_lines: Layer in region intro, boss intro, conquest ritual, music transitions, telegraph VFX, and flashback, victory, and failure stings.
- The vertical slice gains emotional identity beyond functional mechanics.
- Low-HP, boss entry, flashback, and victory moments are materially distinct.
- Depends on: Author Chapter I Crossing Fields content pack
- Depends on: Implement memory mechanics for known-ground slips and reclaim
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: M5 Narrative and audiovisual pass and sections 7.5 to 7.6.

title: Balance and harden 0.1.0 for release candidate
body_lines: Tune damage, pacing thresholds, boss phase lengths, flashback frequency, onboarding, persistence, and telemetry sanity for the MVP release candidate.
- The MVP reaches the acceptance criteria listed in the roadmap.
- The release candidate is stable enough for real users or a real beta.
- Depends on: Add narrative and audiovisual pass for Chapter I
- Depends on: Run MVP feature freeze and hardening pass
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: M6 Balance and hardening, 0.1.0 beta and rc, and Acceptance criteria for 0.1.0.

title: Epic: 0.2.0 more content, same thesis
body_lines: Expand breadth with more regions, bosses, concepts, and trap families without diluting the product identity.
- More concepts, analogies, trap families, and better map reveal.
- Breadth grows while the same thesis still drives content and world design.
- Content consistency remains strong enough to support future personalization and analytics.

title: Epic: 0.3.0 combat depth
body_lines: Make the loop more game-like with shields, mini-bosses, signature attacks, combo or tempo, parry, and reconquest.
- Combo, tempo, and parry or trap-defuse reward.
- The loop feels materially deeper than the MVP and clears the 0.3.0 gate against repetition.

title: Epic: 0.4.0 better pedagogy without softness
body_lines: Strengthen repair, explanation, and anti-trap learning while preserving the severity of the core loop.
- The product gets smarter, not softer, and learning repair becomes materially stronger.

title: Epic: 0.5.0 map recursion and deeper memory palace
body_lines: Push the map from useful interface into unmistakable memory palace with recursion, landmarks, route cues, and cross-region callbacks.
- Stronger landmarks and route memory cues.
- Stronger flashback cinematics and spatial identity.
- The memory palace angle is obvious and clears the 0.5.0 gate.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 0.5.0 Map recursion and deeper memory palace and Gate to 0.5.0.

title: Epic: 0.6.0 personalization
body_lines: Adapt pacing, routing, reclaim frequency, and weak-region flow to the player without destroying fairness.
- User-specific weak-region routing and spine estimation.
- The system adapts meaningfully after the core loops are proven stable.

title: Epic: 0.7.0 social and shareable status
body_lines: Add selective social features that support the fantasy and shareable status without warping the product into a leaderboard farm.
- Defeated boss and region conquered cards.
- Possibly private leaderboards or cohort views later.
- Social features strengthen identity and growth without distorting the core loop.

title: Epic: 0.8.0 predictive and institutional layer
body_lines: Turn the player system into a predictive and institutional wedge with readiness, fail-risk, diagnostics, and dashboard views.
- The data layer is predictive enough to support the 0.8.0 gate and a plausible B2B story.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 0.8.0 Predictive and institutional layer and Gate to 0.8.0.

title: Epic: 0.9.0 product hardening
body_lines: Prepare for 1.0 with performance, content QA at scale, onboarding optimization, monetization integration, tuning, and consistency passes.
- Narrative, art, and audio consistency passes.
- The product is hardened enough that 1.0 can be about completeness rather than obvious instability.

title: Epic: 1.0.0 full product
body_lines: Reach a coherent, recognizably complete expression of the thesis across curriculum, game layer, memory layer, narrative, personalization, social growth, and analytics.
- Personalization, social, and analytics layers brought together coherently.
- The product clears the 1.0.0 gate and feels inevitable rather than stitched together.

title: Cross-cutting epic: stable concept graph and trap taxonomy
body_lines: Stabilize the concept graph, edge taxonomy, and named trap patterns so content, analytics, personalization, and narrative all share the same language.
- Cross-region consistency for content and analytics.
- Later milestones can scale without repeatedly redefining core curriculum structure.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 1.0.0 Curriculum and world, trap taxonomy, and ontology themes.

title: Cross-cutting epic: curriculum and world expansion
body_lines: Expand the curriculum and world together so coverage breadth still feels like a designed Road Master journey.
- Strong analogies and region identities across the world.
- Broad exam coverage still reads as one coherent world rather than disconnected content packs.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 0.2.0 through 1.0.0 world and curriculum expansion sections.

title: Cross-cutting epic: analytics, readiness and fail-risk modeling
body_lines: Build the analytical layer that turns player performance into readiness, fail-risk, diagnostics, and institution-facing views.
- Cohort analytics and educator or school views.
- The analytics layer supports both product optimization and institutional value by 1.0.0.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 0.8.0 Predictive and institutional layer and 1.0.0 Analytics / B2B.

title: Cross-cutting epic: narrative arc, chapters and final gate
body_lines: Carry the Road Master arc from Chapter I to a full chapter progression with titles, ranks, and the final gate.
- Final gate and hero's journey structure.
- The full product has a complete narrative spine instead of isolated thematic slices.
- `chatgpt_conv.md`: 1.0.0 Narrative features and Road Master arc themes.
